metadata.summary: Poetry for dependencies, Django models/views/services, FastAPI routes, pytest fixtures, type hints with mypy --strict, ruff formatting, and asyncio concurrency patterns.

content.title: Python Ecosystem
content.summary: Patterns for the full Python development stack: Poetry for dependency management, Django models/views/services/admin, FastAPI routes/dependencies/schemas, pytest fixtures/mocking/parametrize, type hints with `mypy --strict`, ruff formatting, venv/pyenv, and asyncio concurrency patterns.

why.p: Python projects without a locked dependency manager (`poetry.lock`), type annotations, and enforced formatting accumulate technical debt quickly. The services pattern (thin views + business logic in `services.py`) makes Django/FastAPI code testable from Celery and management commands. `asyncio.gather` reduces I/O-bound latency proportionally to the number of concurrent operations.

list.item: Setting up a new Python project with Poetry and pyproject.toml
Writing Django models, views, serializers, or admin configuration
Creating FastAPI routes, Pydantic schemas, and dependency injection
Writing pytest tests with fixtures, mocking, and parametrize
Adding type hints and running `mypy --strict` on a file or module
Go / Rust / Node.js projects — language-specific skills cover those
Data science / ML workflows — pandas, NumPy, and Jupyter patterns are out of scope here
Legacy Python 2 codebases — methodology assumes Python 3.11+

section.name: Python Project Setup with Poetry

example.title: Poetry initialization and dependency management
example.code:
# New project poetry new my-project && cd my-project
# Add production and dev dependencies poetry add django fastapi poetry add --group dev pytest pytest-django pytest-cov mypy ruff
# Install from lock file (CI) poetry install --no-root
# Run without activating shell poetry run pytest poetry run python manage.py migrate
# Export for Docker poetry export -f requirements.txt --output requirements.txt --without-hashes

example.title: pyenv integration
example.code: # Install and pin Python version for project pyenv install 3.11.7 cd my-project pyenv local 3.11.7 poetry env use $(pyenv which python) poetry install

example.title: Docker pattern
example.code: FROM python:3.11-slim WORKDIR /app RUN pip install poetry COPY pyproject.toml poetry.lock ./ RUN poetry config virtualenvs.create false \ && poetry install --no-interaction --only main COPY . . CMD ["python", "main.py"]

section.reference: Minimal pyproject.toml with dev group and tool stubs

example.title: BaseModel with UUID and timestamps

rule.statement: Business logic lives in services.py, not views. Services receive domain types (email: str, user: User), not request objects. Wrap multi-step DB operations in @transaction.atomic.
rule.rationale: Thin views delegate to services. Services are callable from Celery tasks, management commands, and tests without an HTTP request context.

example.title: Services layer for business logic
FastAPI app with routing and dependencies

section.name: pytest Patterns

example.title: conftest fixtures with factories and mocking

section.reference: Standard pytest conftest with mock_db, api_client, authenticated_client

rule.statement: Use asyncio.gather() for independent concurrent I/O operations. Sequential await calls run one at a time; gather runs them concurrently, reducing total latency proportionally.
rule.rationale: Concurrent I/O patterns minimize wall-clock time when making multiple external API calls or database queries.

example.title: Concurrent I/O with asyncio.gather
example.code:
import asyncio
async def fetch_url(url: str) -> str: # Pseudo-code; use aiohttp in practice return f"Response from {url}"
async def main(): urls = ["http://api1.com", "http://api2.com", "http://api3.com"] results = await asyncio.gather(*[fetch_url(url) for url in urls]) return results
# Run: asyncio.run(main())

example.title: TaskGroup for structured concurrency
example.code:
import asyncio
async def task(name: str, delay: int): await asyncio.sleep(delay) return f"Task {name} done"
async def main(): async with asyncio.TaskGroup() as tg: t1 = tg.create_task(task("A", 1)) t2 = tg.create_task(task("B", 2)) # Both completed or one raised return await t1, await t2

rule.statement: Type the public boundary first: `services/*.py` and route handlers, then internal helpers. Use `X | None` instead of `Optional[X]`. Run `mypy --strict` before commit.
rule.rationale: Public boundaries are where type safety matters most. Strict mode catches edge cases and improves code clarity for reviewers and future maintainers.

example.title: Typed services with mypy strict
example.code:
from typing import TypeVar
T = TypeVar("T")
def get_or_raise(obj: T | None, msg: str) -> T: if obj is None: raise ValueError(msg) return obj
async def fetch_user(user_id: int) -> dict[str, str] | None: # Type-checked return None

rule.statement: Use ruff for linting and formatting. Run `ruff check --fix && ruff format`. Do NOT use Black/isort/flake8 (deprecated); they conflict with ruff.
rule.rationale: ruff combines the functionality of Black, isort, and flake8 into a single, fast tool. Using both stacks causes formatting conflicts.

section.name: Agent Workflow
section.p: Drive Python work as: classify task → pick sub-topic (setup, Django, FastAPI, pytest, or asyncio) → load only the relevant section into context. Execute in a Bash sandbox with `python:*, poetry:*, pytest:*, ruff:*, mypy:*` allow-list. After every code edit, run the project's actual lint command (faion-net = `ruff check --fix && ruff format`), not Black. Commit only after `pytest -x` is green. Never run `poetry update` without explicit user approval — it rewrites the lockfile.

Recommended subagents: faion-code-agent (default executor for Django models/services, FastAPI routes, async handlers), faion-test-agent (owns pytest fixtures, mocking, parametrize, coverage), faion-devtools-developer (sibling skill, owns pre-commit, lint config, Poetry/uv migration), faion-software-architect (sibling, owns Django-vs-FastAPI choice, async/sync boundaries), sdd-execution (wraps the whole loop in spec → impl → test → review for non-trivial tasks).

Prompt pattern for sub-topic dispatch: "Task: <short>. Stack: Django 5 + DRF on Python 3.11. Apply ONLY the 'Django Patterns' section. Constraints: thin views, services for business logic, ruff (not black) for format, type hints required, pytest with @pytest.mark.django_db. Return diff + test run output."

For mypy onboarding (one file at a time): "Add types to apps/<module>/services.py. Use `X | None` not Optional. Update mypy overrides only if a third-party stub is missing. Do NOT touch other files. Run: mypy apps/<module>/services.py — paste full output."

section.name: Best Practices
section.p: Use ruff, not the Black/isort/flake8 (deprecated) trio — faion-net mandates this; the methodology may reference legacy patterns. Pin Python in `.python-version` so CI, Docker, and local dev share a version. Match minor across `pyproject.toml requires-python`, `tool.mypy python_version`, and Dockerfile `FROM python:X.Y`. Services own writes; views own HTTP. Never call `Model.objects.create()` from a view. Never raise `HTTPException` from a service. Type the public boundary first. Async is contagious — don't sprinkle `async` into Django code unless the whole stack (ASGI server, ORM calls via `sync_to_async`, middleware) is ready. Use `select_for_update()` only inside `transaction.atomic()` and only when there's an actual race. Use `uv` for new projects, Poetry only when existing lockfile. uv is dramatically faster and `pip`-compatible. Lock `pyproject.toml` and lockfile together in one commit. Splitting them breaks reproducible builds. Use `pytest -x --lf` during iterative dev (stop on first fail, then run last-failed).

section.name: AI-Agent Gotchas
section.p: README contradicts faion-net repo standard on the linter (Black vs ruff) — trust repo `AGENTS.md` over knowledge base when they disagree. Mixing `Optional[X]` and `X | None` in one file makes mypy noisy and hurts diffs. Pick one per project (modern: `X | None`). Django `transaction.atomic` as decorator vs context manager — only the context-manager form respects custom `using=` databases. Default to context manager. Async DB calls in Django require `sync_to_async`; calling `User.objects.get()` directly inside `async def` raises `SynchronousOnlyOperation`. Easy to miss in code review. `@pytest.mark.django_db` without `transaction=True` rolls back fixtures between tests — but mixing it with `select_for_update` deadlocks under SQLite. Use Postgres for those. `asyncio.gather` swallows exceptions unless `return_exceptions=False` is paired with a try/except around the await. `TaskGroup` is safer; prefer it on 3.11+. `BackgroundTasks` in FastAPI runs in the request lifecycle — long jobs block the worker. Use Celery / arq / dramatiq for >1s work. Test discovery + Django settings: `pytest-django` needs `DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE` set in `pyproject.toml` OR env. Forgetting this causes "Apps aren't loaded yet" with zero useful traceback. Print statements (T20) — ruff blocks `print()` in faion-net code. Use `logging`. Tests are exempt via per-file overrides. Cross-app imports — always alias to avoid circular imports that silently break Django app loading.

section.name: Tools and Services
section.p: Tools: poetry (dep + venv mgmt; lockfile), uv (10-100x faster Poetry alt; pip-compat), ruff (lint + format; replaces black/isort/flake8), mypy (static type checking), pyright (faster mypy alt), pytest + pytest-django + pytest-cov (test runner), pre-commit (git hook orchestrator), pyenv (multiple Python versions), pipx (install Python CLIs in isolated venvs), python -m venv (stdlib venv), safety / pip-audit (CVE scan).

Services: PyPI (OSS registry), ReadTheDocs (OSS docs via sphinx-build CLI), Codecov / Coveralls (SaaS coverage tracking), Sentry (SaaS APM via sentry-sdk + middleware), Render / Fly.io / Railway (SaaS PaaS with automatic pyproject.toml detection).

template.purpose: Minimal Poetry pyproject.toml with dev group and tool stubs
Standard pytest conftest with mock_db, api_client, authenticated_client
[tool.pytest.ini_options] block with Django settings and addopts
